methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $43M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median

methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Tevel Aerobotics is a Series B autonomous systems company in the Drones & Autonomous sector that has raised $42.6M to date. The valuation comp universe includes seven private peers in drones and autonomous systems (Elroy Air, Joby Aviation, Nuro, Archer Aviation, Manna Aero among others), though no direct public comparables exist in the narrow category.

The stage-progression method is the primary applicable triangulation approach here. Using total capital raised of $43M as a baseline proxy and applying a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B-to-next-stage progression, the analysis implies a valuation of $81M. A peer-median approach was not applied due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables within acceptable filtering parameters; the broader peer set lacks sufficient homogeneity to support reliable median benchmarking without introducing noise.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $69M (low) to $93M (high), with a median point of $81M. This range reflects inherent uncertainty around the appropriate step-up multiple and the absence of tightly comparable transactions. Key sensitivities include the validity of the 1.9x multiplier for this specific company's stage and sector dynamics, execution progress since last funding event, and market conditions for autonomous systems funding. Given the limited data signals, investors should treat this range as directional rather than definitive and require additional data (revenue, customer validation, burn rate) to narrow bands.
